source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of America Region, Garden Banks Block 215

event.top_event: High-pressure hose rupture with personnel injury
actions_taken: Medical evaluation of three injured personnel by Rig Medic
Two personnel sent for onshore medical evaluation on 17 July 2025
One personnel reevaluated and sent for further evaluation on 18 July 2025
0.375-inch high-pressure hose submitted to laboratory for forensic analysis
BSEE onsite Incident Follow-up Investigation conducted on 21 July 2025
All personnel received full return to work release
event.summary: On 17 July 2025 at 0752 hours during workover operations at Garden Banks Block 215, a 0.375-inch high-pressure hose ruptured while blowing down a coil tubing reel with nitrogen. The rupture struck and injured three personnel: a Noble Assistant Driller on the left arm, an Oil States employee on the left side of the face, and a Schlumberger Operator who injured his knee while avoiding the hose. Laboratory analysis determined the hose failed due to chlorides corrosion coupled with rapid oxidation of wire reinforcement strands. The incident was classified as First Aid Only with no medical attention required beyond initial evaluation.

hazards.name: High-pressure fluid energy in hose systems
Corrosion and material degradation of pressure equipment

threats.name: Hose rupture due to internal corrosion and wire mesh oxidation
Uncontrolled release of pressurized fluid and hose whip

consequences.name: Personnel struck by ruptured hose causing injury

controls.name: Safety restraints and whip checks on high-pressure hose
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel injury from hose whip

supporting_text: There were no safety restraints or whip checks installed on the high-pressure hose as recommended in Oil States Standard Operation Procedure for Flow Iron and Restraint Installation
when the 0.375-inch high-pressure hose ruptured, it struck and injured two of the three employees

controls.name: Hose inspection procedure
controls.barrier_role: Detect hose degradation and corrosion before failure

supporting_text: There is no formal hose inspection procedure in place

the BSEE Incident Investigation Team also reviewed the operator's reports and concurred with the operator's findings that there was no established hose inspection procedure or maintenance program in place

controls.name: Preventative maintenance program for high-pressure hoses
controls.barrier_role: Maintain hose integrity and prevent corrosion-induced failure

supporting_text: There was no preventative maintenance program to evaluate the hose's operational condition
the hose failed due to internal corrosion along with rapid oxidation of the wire mesh reinforcement strands

controls.name: Hose material selection and design standards
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hose failure through appropriate material selection resistant to corrosion

supporting_text: Laboratory analysis of the 0.375-inch high-pressure hose determined that the hose ruptured due to chlorides corrosion coupled with rapid oxidation of wire reinforcement strands

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
